description: This figure presents a comprehensive TEM investigation of the microstructural evolution in Fe-20Mn-9Al-1.5C-3Cr (3Cr) austenitic low-density steel during tensile deformation at various engineering strains. The bright-field images were captured along the [011]γ zone axis of the FCC matrix, with selected area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns inset confirming the crystal structure. Subfigures (a)-(c) at 3% strain reveal planar dislocation slip characterized by dislocation multipoles localized within {111} slip bands and constrained dislocations within (1-11) planar slip bands, indicating strong slip planarity with a Schmid factor of 0.52. Subfigures (d)-(f) at 10% strain demonstrate increased dislocation density with narrower slip band spacing, showing the operation of multiple slip systems including (1-11) and (11-1) slip bands carrying high-density dislocation arrays (HDDA). Subfigures (g)-(l) near final failure (54% strain, labeled LD) exhibit high-density dislocations, very fine slip bands, and the late formation of deformation twins (~dozen nanometres width) and stacking faults (~dozen nanometres width) observed via bright-field, high-resolution, and dark-field imaging. The evolution demonstrates that 3Cr steel is dominated by HDDA and dislocation slip throughout deformation, with strain hardening primarily contributed by HDDA densification and dynamic slip band refinement (DSBR), while deformation twins appear only at very high strain levels near fracture with limited contribution to overall ductility.
